twoSumII.title: Two Sum II - Input Array Is Sorted
twoSumII.desc: Given a 1-indexed array of integers numbers that is already sorted in non-decreasing order, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target number. Let these two numbers be numbers[index1] and numbers[index2] where 1 <= index1 < index2 <= numbers.length. Return the indices of the two numbers, index1 and index2, added by one as an integer array [index1, index2] of length 2. The tests are generated such that there is exactly one solution. You may not use the same element twice. Your solution must use only constant extra space.

example.explanation: The sum of 2 and 7 is 9. Since the array is 1-indexed, index1 = 1, index2 = 2. We return [1, 2].

constraints: numbers is sorted in non-decreasing order.

palindrome.title: Valid Palindrome
palindrome.desc: A phrase is a palindrome if, after converting all uppercase letters into lowercase letters and removing all non-alphanumeric characters, it reads the same forward and backward. Alphanumeric characters include letters and numbers. Given a string s, return true if it is a palindrome, or false otherwise.

example.input: s = "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
example.explanation: "amanaplanacanalpanama" is a palindrome after removing non-alphanumeric characters and converting to lowercase.

constraints: s consists only of printable ASCII characters.

threeSum.title: 3Sum
threeSum.desc: Given an integer array nums, return all the triplets [nums[i], nums[j], nums[k]] such that i != j, i != k, and j != k, and nums[i] + nums[j] + nums[k] == 0. Notice that the solution set must not contain duplicate triplets.

example.explanation: The distinct triplets are [-1, -1, 2] and [-1, 0, 1]. Note that the order of the output and the order of the triplets does not matter.

trappingRain.title: Trapping Rain Water
trappingRain.desc: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.

example.explanation: The above elevation map (black section) is represented by array [0,1,0,2,1,0,1,3,2,1,2,1]. In this case, 6 units of rain water (blue section) are being trapped.

linkedListCycle.title: Linked List Cycle
linkedListCycle.desc: Given head, the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. There is a cycle in a linked list if there is some node in the list that can be reached again by continuously following the next pointer. Return true if there is a cycle in the linked list. Otherwise, return false.

example.explanation: There is a cycle in the linked list, where the tail connects to the 1st node (0-indexed, value 2).

constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 10^4].
pos is -1 or a valid index in the linked-list.

maxAvgSubarray.title: Maximum Average Subarray I
maxAvgSubarray.desc: You are given an integer array nums consisting of n elements, and an integer k. Find a contiguous subarray whose length is equal to k that has the maximum average value and return this value. Any answer with a calculation error less than 10^-5 will be accepted.

maxVowels.title: Maximum Number of Vowels in a Substring of Given Length
maxVowels.desc: Given a string s and an integer k, return the maximum number of vowel letters in any substring of s with length k. Vowel letters in English are 'a', 'e', 'i', 'o', and 'u'.

example.explanation: The substring "iii" contains 3 vowel letters.

constraints: s consists of lowercase English letters.

longestNoRepeat.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
longestNoRepeat.desc: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

example.explanation: The answer is "abc", with the length of 3.

constraints: s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces.

minWindowSubstring.title: Minimum Window Substring
minWindowSubstring.desc: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window. If there is no such substring, return the empty string "".

example.explanation: The minimum window substring "BANC" includes 'A', 'B', and 'C' from string t.

constraints: s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters.

binarySearch.title: Binary Search
binarySearch.desc: Given an array of integers nums which is sorted in ascending order, and an integer target, write a function to search target in nums. If target exists, then return its index. Otherwise, return -1. You must write an algorithm with O(log n) runtime complexity.

example.explanation: 9 exists in nums and its index is 4

constraints: All the integers in nums are unique.
nums is sorted in ascending order.

searchInRotatedSortedArray.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
searchInRotatedSortedArray.desc: There is an integer array nums sorted in ascending order (with distinct values). Prior to being passed to your function, nums is possibly rotated at an unknown pivot index k (1 <= k < nums.length) such that the resulting array is [nums[k], nums[k+1], ..., nums[n-1], nums[0], nums[1], ..., nums[k-1]] (0-indexed). Given the array nums after the possible rotation and an integer target, return the index of target if it is in nums, or -1 if it is not in nums. You must write an algorithm with O(log n) runtime complexity.

example.explanation: 0 is found at index 4

constraints: All values of nums are unique.
nums is an ascending array that is possibly rotated.

medianOfTwoSortedArrays.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
medianOfTwoSortedArrays.desc: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n respectively, return the median of the two sorted arrays. The overall run time complexity should be O(log (m+n)).

example.explanation: merged array = [1,2,3] and median is 2.

twoSum.title: Two Sum
twoSum.desc: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target. You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

example.explanation: Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, we return [0, 1].

groupAnagrams.title: Group Anagrams
groupAnagrams.desc: Given an array of strings strs, group the anagrams together. You can return the answer in any order. An Anagram is a word or phrase formed by rearranging the letters of a different word or phrase, typically using all the original letters exactly once.

example.explanation: Words that are anagrams of each other are grouped together.

constraints: strs[i] consists of lowercase English letters.

validSudoku.title: Valid Sudoku
validSudoku.desc: Determine if a 9 x 9 Sudoku board is valid. Only the filled cells need to be validated according to the following rules: Each row must contain the digits 1-9 without repetition. Each column must contain the digits 1-9 without repetition. Each of the nine 3 x 3 sub-boxes of the grid must contain the digits 1-9 without repetition.

example.explanation: All rows, columns, and 3x3 boxes contain no duplicate digits.

subarraySum.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
subarraySum.desc: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the total number of subarrays whose sum equals to k. A subarray is a contiguous non-empty sequence of elements within an array.

example.explanation: The subarrays [1,1] (index 0-1) and [1,1] (index 1-2) both sum to 2.

subsets.title: Subsets
subsets.desc: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.
constraints: All the numbers of nums are unique.

permutations.title: Permutations
permutations.desc: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all the possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.
constraints: All the integers of nums are unique.

combinationSum.title: Combination Sum
combinationSum.desc: Given an array of distinct integers candidates and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target. You may return the combinations in any order.
constraints: All elements of candidates are distinct.

nQueens.title: N-Queens
nQueens.desc: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Given an integer n, return all distinct solutions to the n-queens puzzle. You may return the answer in any order.

maxDepth.title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
maxDepth.desc: Find the maximum depth of a binary tree.

example.explanation: The longest path from root to leaf is 3 -> 20 -> 15 (or 7) which has 3 nodes.

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4]

invertTree.title: Invert Binary Tree
invertTree.desc: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree, and return its root.

example.explanation: Left and right children of all nodes are swapped.

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100]

levelOrder.title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
levelOrder.desc: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes values.

example.explanation: Level 0 is [3], Level 1 is [9, 20], Level 2 is [15, 7].

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000]

lca.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
lca.desc: Find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) node of two given nodes in the BST.

example.explanation: The LCA of nodes 2 and 8 is 6.

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [2, 10^5]
p and q will exist in the BST.
